title: Properties of i
text: Recall $$i^1=i$$, $$i^2=-1$$, $$i^3=-i$$, $$i^4=1$$. This repeats every four powers, i.e. $$i^5=i$$, and so on.

title: Simplifying the First Term
text: $$\frac{1}{i}$$ simplifies to -i. How? Multiply the numerator and denominator by i, then use the first hint.

title: Simplifying the Second Term
text: Similarly, use the first hint on $$\frac{4}{i^3}$$. This gets us $$\frac{-4}{i}$$. To remove i from the denominator we multiply the numerator and denominator by i to get $$\frac{-4i}{i^2}$$. Use the first hint again to simplify.

title: Getting the Full Equation
text: Now, add the imaginary numbers together to get the answer.
